DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF JUSTIN PHAM'S MOTION COMPELLING FOR ORDER COMPELLING ANSWERS TO INTERROGATORIES AND COMPELLING RESPONSE TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT ADMITS THAT IT FAILED TO PROVIDE ANY RESPONSES TO THE DISCOVERY AT ISSUE. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO RESPOND TO THE DISCOVERY AT ISSUE, WITHOUT OBJECTION, WITHIN SEVEN CALENDAR DAYS OF THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY TO PERPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 BY EMAIL TO JUDGE PRO TEM TONY SCHOENBERG AT TSCHOENBERG@FBM.COM. Notice Of Motion And Motion Compelling For Order Compelling Answers To Interrogatories And Compelling Response To Request For Production Of Documents And Sanctions; Points And Authorities; Declaration Of John P. Strouss Iii
Off Calendar Oct-29-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
John P. Strouss III, Esq., Law Offices of John P Strouss, III 415-771-6174, appearing for Plaintiff Justin Pham; (CourtCall)
Mark Rennie, Esq., Attorney at Law, 415-981-4500, appearing for Defendant Zen Compound LLC
Jamie C. Lee, Esq., Springel & Finnk LLP, 714-957-5742, appearing for Defendant Security Intelligence Specialist Corporation; (CourtCall)

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JUSTIN PHAM'S Motion Compelling For Order Compelling Answers To Interrogatories And Compelling Response To Request For Production Of Documents And Sanctions

Plaintiff's Motion to Compel is granted. Defendant admits that it failed to provide any responses to the discovery at issue. Defendant is ordered to respond to the discovery at issue, without objection, within seven calendar days of the date of this order. Request for sanctions are denied. Plaintiff/moving party to perpare and submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 by email to Judge Pro Tem Tony Schoenberg at tschoenberg@fbm.com. Judge Pro Tem: Tony Schoenber; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)
